_comment: Binaries CI provisions for instances that are NOT container-first. An instance is
container-first when it can source a node from an image — either the chain's own
official image pinned to the live-network version (e.g. xplad), or one starskiff
builds and publishes itself (config/images.json, e.g. evmd). Everything below sources
from a binary instead, for the reason on each entry.
release-binaries.yml builds/uploads these once; ci.yml downloads them per run.
Re-check each `reason` on bump — if the image situation changed, move the chain to a
container-first instance (official image) or to config/images.json (we publish it).

binaries.name: simd
binaries.reason: ghcr.io/cosmos/simapp only tags minor lines (v0.53, v0.54) — no patch-level pin

binaries.name: wasmd
binaries.reason: cosmwasm/wasmd publishes exact tags — candidate for a container-first instance

binaries.name: evmd
binaries.reason: TRANSITIONAL binary lane: evmd is now container-first on the image we publish (config/images.json). Kept here only so the integration harness (global-setup pins binary:'evmd') stays green until that image is published+digest-pinned; drop this entry then.

binaries.name: gaiad
binaries.reason: ghcr.io/cosmos/gaia lags mainnet (latest tags v25/v26 vs live v27.5.0)

binaries.name: hermes
binaries.reason: relayer, not a chain — the hermes instance drives the CLI and its config files directly
